note_en:
, creator and publisher of the massively popular “ ” game, announced a $1.78 billion round of funding — which the company says gives it a post-money equity valuation of $17.3 billion.
 The round includes the  previously announced $250 million strategic investment from Sony Corp., under which   obtained a 1.4% stake in Epic. Following the closing of the funding round, Epic will remain controlled by founder and CEO Tim Sweeney.
 Additional investors in Epic’s epic fundraise include Baillie Gifford, funds and accounts managed by BlackRock, Fidelity Management & Research Co., Lightspeed Venture Partners, Ontario Teachers’ Pension Plan Board, funds and accounts advised by T. Rowe Price Associates, and billionaire hedge-fund manager David Tepper.
  Existing investors KKR and Smash Ventures also participated in the round, adding to their 2018 investment. Epic Games in October 2018 announced $1.25 billion in funding from KKR, Smash Ventures, Iconiq Capital, aXiomatic, Vulcan Capital, Kleiner Perkins and Lightspeed. In 2012, Chinese internet giant Tencent invested $330 million in Epic.
  “Having the support of leaders in the financial community accelerates Epic’s efforts to build a new kind of digital ecosystem using real-time 3D technology, services that connect hundreds of millions of people, and a digital storefront that offers a fair business model. We are delighted to have them as part of the Epic family,” Sweeney said in a statement.
 Credit Suisse and the Raine Group acted as joint placement agents to Epic, while law firm Wilson Sonsini Goodrich & Rosati provided legal counsel to Epic.
 Mega-popular battle royale title “Fortnite” has been building itself into more of an entertainment destination with the game’s Party Royale, which Epic Games calls “a new experimental and evolving space.” That has included  screenings of three Christopher Nolan films on June 26 under an agreement with Warner Bros., and “Fortnite” also has featured virtual concerts from artists including Travis Scott — who  broke attendance records —  Marshmello and Deadmau5.
 This past May, Epic Games said that “Fortnite,” a free-to-play game with in-app purchases and subscription options, topped 350 million players worldwide. In 2019,  “Fortnite” raked in $1.8 billion, making it the No. 1 game title worldwide in terms of revenue, according to research firm SuperData.
title: EPIC Games筹集了17.8亿美元，其中包括上月宣布的索尼2.5亿美元，融资后估值为173亿美元
title_en: Epic Games raises $1.78B, which includes $250M from Sony announced last month, at a post-money valuation of $17.3B

note:
连载小说应用Radish在软银亚洲风险投资(SoftBank Ventures Asia)和Kakao Page的牵头下，获得了6320万美元的首轮融资，以进入好莱坞的讲故事社区。
联合创始人Seung Yoon Lee告诉Crunchbase News，新的投资使Radish的总资金超过6800万美元。根据Crunchbase的数据，该公司上一次筹集资金是在2017年，包括300万美元的种子轮。
这家初创公司将利用这笔新资金来充实其领导团队，聘请更多的作家和数据科学家，并让其新的洛杉矶办事处投入运营。浪漫故事是最热门的类型之一，但拉迪什想要扩展到恐怖、神秘和惊险。
“使用数据驱动的产品，我们通过测试封面、标题和试点来找出你想要什么，当我们找出读者参与的是什么时，我们会很快给他们，”李补充道。“因为我们既是一家科技公司，也是一家内容和媒体公司，我们还希望扩大我们的内容库，同时在营销和数据方面花钱，以便分发给更广泛的受众。”
这个总部设在纽约和首尔的移动小说平台是李开复四年前创立的，旨在通过智能手机提供一小块大小的故事供阅读。萝卜还制作萝卜原创，由艾美奖获奖肥皂剧编剧、作家和萝卜的内容团队创作者撰写的小而易读的剧集。
“你可以把萝卜想象成HBO或Netflix的连载小说，”李说。“故事还在继续，而且在不断更新。我们关注的是内容狂欢的行为，我们在某些方面把这一点发挥到了极致。“。
读者可以访问数千部跨流派的连续剧，每部只需20美分-如果他们愿意等待，也可以免费-并在现场社区聊天室直接与最喜欢的讲故事的人联系，而业余作者可以通过用户提交的内容发布自己的原创故事。
Rish已经在洛杉矶开设了一个新的办事处，以招聘新的人才，根据目标类型建立新的热门内容系列，将其原始IP改编成不同形式的多媒体娱乐，如游戏和电视，并专注于为美国读者扩大绩效营销。
就增长而言，该公司的收入在过去一年里增长了25倍，达到每天超过10万美元，该平台已经制作了30部原创电视剧的6500多集。与此同时，据该公司称，顶级萝卜系列在短短几个季度内就赚了400多万美元，累计阅读量超过5000万次。
Kakao Page首席执行官李振洙(jin-soo Lee)在一份书面声明中表示，“全球移动小说市场具有令人兴奋的增长潜力，我们将与萝卜一起积极寻找和发现各种令人兴奋的故事。”
与此同时，软银风险投资亚洲公司(SoftBank Ventures Asia)首席执行官兼管理合伙人JP·李(JP Lee)在一份书面声明中表示，萝卜已经表明，它可以改变人们在手机上消费内容的方式。
他补充说：“凭借自己快节奏的原创内容制作，萝卜最有可能成为全球在线小说市场的领先者。”
与此同时，拉迪什专注于增加一些领导职位，包括一名创意总监和负责内容的副总裁，以及内容、营销、数据科学家和金融专家。
该公司现在有45名员工，李开复预计到今年年底将有大约70名员工。
“在过去的一年里，我们增长很快，有些故事每天赚2万美元，”李说。“我们希望容纳更大的内容库，并在营销上投入更多资金，因此我们希望立即填补这些领导角色的空缺。”
note_en:
Serialized fiction app  Radish landed $63.2 million in Series A funding, led by  SoftBank Ventures Asia and  Kakao Page, to tap into Hollywood’s storytelling community.
  The new investment brings Radish’s total funding to more than $68 million, co-founder  Seung Yoon Lee told Crunchbase News. The company last raised funds in 2017, including a  $3 million seed round, according to Crunchbase data.
 The startup will use the new funds to round out its leadership team, hire more writers and data scientists, and get its new Los Angeles office running. Romance is one of the top genres, but Radish would like to expand to horror, mystery and thriller.
 “Using a data-driven production, we figure out what you want by testing covers, titles and pilots, and when we figure out what readers engage with, we give that to them fast,” Lee added. “Because we are a content and media company, as much as a tech company, we also want to expand our content library, as well as spend money in marketing and data to distribute to a wider audience.”
  The mobile fiction platform, based in New York and Seoul, was founded by Lee four years ago to provide bite-sized stories for reading via smartphone. Radish also produces Radish Originals, small, digestible episodes written by Emmy Award-winning soap opera writers, authors and Radish’s content team creators.
 “You can think of Radish as an HBO or Netflix for serialized fiction,” Lee said. “The stories are ongoing and are constantly updated. We are focusing on the behavior of binging content, and we take that to an extreme in some ways.”
 Readers can access thousands of serials across genres for as little as 20 cents each—or free if they are willing to wait—and connect directly with favorite storytellers in live community chat rooms, while amateur authors can publish their own original stories via user-submitted content.
 Radish has opened a new office in Los Angeles in order to hire new talent, build new hit content series based on targeted genres, adapt its original IPs into different forms of multimedia entertainment such as gaming and TV, and focus on scaling performance marketing for U.S. readers.
 In terms of growth, the company’s revenue has grown 25 times over the past year, to more than $100,000 daily, and the platform has produced more than 6,500 episodes across 30 original series. Meanwhile, the top Radish series has made more than $4 million in just a few quarters and amassed more than 50 million cumulative reads, according to the company.
  Jin-soo Lee, CEO of Kakao Page, said in a written statement that there is “exciting growth potential of the global mobile fiction market and we will actively source and discover a diverse range of exciting stories together with Radish.”
 Meanwhile,  JP Lee, CEO and managing partner of SoftBank Ventures Asia, said in a written statement that Radish has shown it can change the way people are consuming content on their phones.
 “With its own fast-paced original content production, Radish is best positioned to become a leading player in the global online fiction market,” he added.
  Meanwhile, Radish is focused on adding some leadership positions, including a creative director and vice president of content, as well as content, marketing, data scientists and finance experts.
 The company has 45 employees now, and Lee expects to have approximately 70 by the end of the year.
 “We’ve grown fast in the past year, some of the stories are making $20,000 per day,” Lee said. “We want to accommodate a bigger content library and spend more on marketing, so we want to fill in those leadership roles immediately.”
title: 连载小说应用Radish首轮融资6,320万美元，由软银亚洲风险投资公司(SoftBank Ventures Asia)和Kakao Page牵头
title_en: Serialized fiction app Radish raises $63.2M Series A led by SoftBank Ventures Asia and Kakao Page

note:
任天堂公布4-6月当季营业利润为1,447亿日圆(14亿美元)，超出分析师预期。
该公司广受欢迎的任天堂Switch和Switch Lite游戏机的销量在本季度增长了约167%，达到568万台。
该公司目前已售出2240万册“穿越动物：新地平线”，超过超级粉碎兄弟终极版的销量。
任天堂周四表示，该公司第一财季营业利润猛增428%，这一猛增反映出视频游戏行业从冠状病毒大流行中获益良多。
这家日本游戏巨头公布第二季度营业利润为1447亿日元(合14亿美元)，打破了分析师的预期，与去年同期的274亿日元相比大幅攀升。
与此同时，任天堂的净销售额为3581亿日圆，比去年同期的1721亿日圆增长了108%。该公司广受欢迎的任天堂Switch和Switch Lite游戏机的销量在本季度增长了约167%，达到568万台。
这些数字突显了冠状病毒封锁期间游戏需求的繁荣。本周，动视暴雪(Activision Blizzard)和Take-Two Interactive等大型游戏发行商公布了稳健的业绩。
任天堂尤其从这场大流行中获得了提振。3月份发行的“穿越动物：新地平线”一书迅速走红，玩家们在室内寻找一种逃避现实的方式。
该公司目前已售出2240万份这款社交模拟游戏，销量几乎比上一季度翻了一番，超过了2018年发布的超级粉碎兄弟终极版(Super Smash Bros.Ultimate)的销量。任天堂软件的数字销售额攀升了约230%，占软件总销售额的56%左右。
日本同业索尼本周稍早公布第一季财报，获利下滑1%，亦优于分析师预期。尽管销量下滑，但该公司也从游戏中获得提振，游戏销量同比攀升82%，达到9100万台。在售出的游戏总数中，创纪录的74%来自数字下载。
note_en:
Nintendo reported operating profit of 144.7 billion yen ($1.4 billion) in the April-June quarter, smashing analyst expectations.
 Sales of the company's popular Nintendo Switch and Switch Lite consoles grew around 167% to 5.68 million units in the quarter.
 The firm has now sold 22.4 million copies of "Animal Crossing: New Horizons," surpassing sales of "Super Smash Bros. Ultimate."
 Nintendo's operating profits skyrocketed 428% in the fiscal first quarter, the company said Thursday, a wild jump that reflects just how much the video game industry has benefited from the coronavirus pandemic.
  The Japanese gaming giant reported operating profit of 144.7 billion yen ($1.4 billion) in the April-June quarter, smashing analyst expectations and representing a massive climb from the 27.4 billion yen it posted in the year-ago quarter.
 Net sales meanwhile came in at 358.1 billion yen, up an impressive 108% from the 172.1 billion yen Nintendo reported in the same period last year. Sales of the company's popular Nintendo Switch and Switch Lite consoles grew around 167% to 5.68 million units in the quarter.
  The numbers highlight the boom in demand for gaming during coronavirus lockdowns. Big game publishers like Activision Blizzard and Take-Two Interactive have reported  solid results this week.
 Nintendo in particular has gotten a boost from the pandemic. Its "Animal Crossing: New Horizons" title released in March quickly became a hit as players looked for a form of escapism while stuck indoors.
 The company has now sold 22.4 million copies of the social simulation game, with units almost doubling from the previous quarter and surpassing sales of "Super Smash Bros. Ultimate" which was released in 2018. Digital sales of software climbed about 230% for Nintendo and accounted for about 56% of total software sales.
  Japanese peer  Sony reported a 1% drop in profits in its first-quarter results earlier this week, also beating analyst expectations. Despite that decline, the company  also got a boost from gaming, with game sales climbing 82% year-on-year to 91 million units. Of the total number of games sold, a record 74% were from digital downloads.
title: 任天堂公布季度营业利润为14亿美元，同比增长428%；任天堂Switch和Switch Lite游戏机的销量同比增长167%，达到568万台
title_en: Nintendo reported quarterly operating profit of $1.4B, up 428% YoY; sales of Nintendo Switch and Switch Lite consoles grew 167% YoY to 5.68M units

note:
三星应该把这些东西叫做Galaxy Beans。这是多么错失的机会啊。说到这里，让我们来谈谈新的“银河花蕾现场”(Galaxy Buds Live)。在过去的几个月里，该公司最新的真正的无线耳机及其非传统的设计已经广泛泄露，在此过程中我有很多问题。如何使用耳塞不能密封的耳塞来实现噪音消除？这个豆子形状可能舒服吗？
售价169.99美元的Buds Live有自己的风格。它们在古铜色中反光超强，在某些灯光下看起来更像玫瑰金。但我总体上喜欢三星正在试验的外观和事实。你从来没有戴过这样的耳塞，这意味着头几次戴耳机感觉有点陌生，你可能会想太多。事实证明，它们很自然地插进了我的耳甲--豆子的设计很管用！--而且放在那里既舒适又稳定。没有硅胶耳尖，扬声器直接指向你的耳道。Buds Live在顶部附近有一些硅胶稳定器，如果能帮助芽更好地位于耳朵的轮廓中，盒子里还有一套稍大的稳定器。
我的朋友梅根耳朵很小，很难让Galaxy Buds Live合身。我没有这样的麻烦，我是那些耳朵讨厌普通AirPods的人之一；这里的适合是不同的，完全是独一无二的。我发现最好的音质和低音表现是当我把它们尽可能舒适地塞进耳朵里的时候。
轻触控制的工作原理与Galaxy Buds Plus上的类似：轻触可暂停，轻触两次可跳过，轻触三次可返回，您还可以自定义长按的功能。Galaxy Buds Live手提箱有一个USB-C端口和无线充电支持，非常紧凑，但如果你把它掉下来，耳机肯定会飞出来，因为它们的底座不是很深。
AKG调谐的Buds Live有12毫米的驱动器-比Galaxy Buds Plus内部的驱动器还大-以及一个“低音管道”，帮助提升低端。与AirPods Pro和谷歌的Pixel Buds一样，三星也内置了通风口，让它们感觉尽可能自然，而不会有任何被封闭的感觉。这种宽敞的音质非常适合动感十足、酒体饱满的音质。我对Galaxy Buds Live音效的好听感到惊喜。泰勒·斯威夫特(Taylor Swift)与邦·艾弗(Bon Iver)的“流放”合作是这些萌芽的一个很好的展示，斯威夫特和贾斯汀·弗农(Justin Vernon)鲜明的嗓音品质都清晰地展现了出来。
同样，杰克·安东诺夫(Jack Antonoff)在新专辑“小鸡的汽油打火机”(Gaslightter By The Chicks)中的作品也有足够的呼吸空间，和声从不会感觉混杂在一起。跳过不同的歌单和类型，我对Buds Live的温暖和平衡感到非常高兴。如果你担心拜斯，不用担心。只要它们在你的耳朵里，Buds Live就能在底部踢出大量的魅力。这些听起来很棒，考虑到170美元的价格，这正是我所希望的。
但Galaxy Buds Live在噪音消除方面有点超卖。非常清楚的是，这些耳塞在任何时候都会让大量的外界噪音进入。这是故意的，我认为这个名字的“Live”部分就是从这里来的。当我们有一天回到办公室时，这些不会淹没你的同事，他们也不会成为你首选的飞机伴侣。三星表示，它设计的Buds Live消除了非常低的频率-想想空调持续的嗡嗡声或美国铁路公司(Amtrak)的隆隆声-当你打开有源噪声消除时，你可以听到一些环境声音被切断。
但AirPods Pro或索尼1000XM3并非如此。你还是会听到很多你周围的世界。有些人会喜欢这样，他们不必担心错过旅行通知或有人试图在附近与他们交谈，但当我听音乐时，我更像是一个对其他一切都不屑一顾的人。如果是你，Galaxy Buds Plus可能是更好的选择，因为它们会密封你的耳朵，创造更多的隔离，即使没有任何有源噪音消除。如果你因为广告中的噪音取消而购买了Galaxy Buds Live，你会非常失望和失望。
Buds Live的蓝牙稳定性似乎不如Galaxy Buds Plus强大。我在街上走的时候偶尔会突然断线。这并不是经常令人沮丧的原因，但它比我最近评论过的其他耳塞发生的频率更高。不过，当我在公寓放松和聆听时，信号没有任何问题。我对电池续航时间也没有任何不满；三星表示，Buds Live在启用ANC的情况下可以持续长达6小时的连续收听(充电后总计21小时)，在关闭的情况下可以持续8小时(带保护套的情况下为29小时)。这些数字相当不错，击败了AirPods Pro，但还不到Galaxy Buds Plus充电可以持续的惊人的11个小时。
一共有三个波束形成麦克风-两个在外部，一个在内部-Galaxy Buds Live在我的Zoom会议和语音通话中表现得很好。三星表示，他们的硬件中也有一个“语音拾取单元”，用于改进语音检测。你可以单独使用其中一个插座，而另一个插座则在箱子里充电。
所以有很多好处，但是“银河花蕾直播”也不是没有缺点。首先，它们的耐水性和耐汗性只被评为IPX2，这一点不如2020年其他制造商的无线耳塞耐用。我会有点担心他们在雨中，或者如果你在健身房出了很多汗。这些并不是注定要成为健身花蕾的，这是令人失望的，因为它们的设计可以帮助你在外出跑步时保持清醒。与Jabra以外的大多数其他真正的无线耳塞一样，Buds Live一次只能连接一个设备。
作为一个整体，Galaxy Buds Live超出了我的预期，因为泄露的信息，以及我对豆子形状对舒适性和音质的影响的不确定性。在实际使用它们之后，这两个都不会成为一个令人担忧的问题。如果你在收听“银河百货现场”时能够忍受健康剂量的外界声音，那么这里有很多值得你喜欢的地方。
note_en:
Samsung should’ve called these things the Galaxy Beans. What a missed opportunity. With that out of the way, let’s talk about the new Galaxy Buds Live. The company’s latest true wireless earbuds and their unconventional design have leaked extensively over the last few months, and I’ve had many questions along the way. How do you achieve noise cancellation with earbuds that don’t seal in your ears? Can this bean shape possibly feel comfortable?
 The $169.99 Buds Live have a style all their own. They’re super reflective in the bronze color, which can look more rose gold in some lighting. But I generally like the look and the fact that Samsung is experimenting. You’ve never worn earbuds quite like these, which means putting them in the first few times feels a bit foreign, and you might overthink it. Turns out, they slot into the concha of my ears quite naturally — the bean design works! — and are both comfortable and stable once there. There are no silicone tips; the speaker points directly into your ear canal. The Buds Live have some silicone stabilizers near the top, and there’s a slightly larger set included in the box if those help the buds better sit in your ears’ contours.
  My friend Megan has very small ears and had a difficult time getting the Galaxy Buds Live to fit. I had no such trouble, and I’m one of those people whose ears  hate regular AirPods; the fit here is different and wholly unique. I found that the best sound quality and bass performance came when I shoved them as far into my ears as they’d comfortably go.
  The tap controls work just like those on the Galaxy Buds Plus: single tap to pause, double to skip, three to go back, and you can customize what a long press does. The Galaxy Buds Live carrying case, which has a USB-C port and wireless charging support, is wonderfully compact, though the earbuds will definitely go flying out if you drop it since they don’t have a very deep cradle.
 The AKG-tuned Buds Live have 12-millimeter drivers — larger than what’s inside the Galaxy Buds Plus — and a “bass duct” to help ramp up the low end. Like the AirPods Pro and Google’s Pixel Buds, Samsung also built in an air vent to make them feel as natural as possible without any sensation of being closed off. This spaciousness lends well to audio quality, which is dynamic and full-bodied. I’ve been pleasantly surprised by just how good the Galaxy Buds Live sound. Taylor Swift’s “Exile” collaboration with Bon Iver is a great showcase for these buds, with the distinct vocal qualities of Swift and Justin Vernon both coming through clearly.
 Similarly, Jack Antonoff’s production on the new album  Gaslighter by The Chicks has plenty of room to breathe, and the harmonies never feel mushed together. Jumping across different playlists and genres, I came away very pleased by the warmth and balance of the Buds Live. If you’re worried about bass, don’t be. So long as they’re in your ears right, the Buds Live can kick out plenty of oomph on the bottom end. These sound excellent and just what I’d hoped for considering the $170 price.
  But the noise cancellation aspect of the Galaxy Buds Live is being oversold a bit. To be very clear, these earbuds let in a significant amount of outside noise  at all times. That’s intentional and where I think the “Live” part of the name comes from. These aren’t going to drown out your co-workers when we get back to the office someday, nor are they going to be your preferred airplane companions. Samsung says it designed the Buds Live to eliminate very low frequencies — think the persistent hum of an air conditioner or the rumbling of an Amtrak — and you  can hear some of that ambient sound get sliced off when you turn on active noise cancellation.
   But AirPods Pro or Sony 1000XM3s these are not. You’re still going to hear plenty of the world around you. Some people will like that they won’t have to worry about missing travel announcements or someone trying to speak to them nearby, but I’m more of a tune-out-everything-else person when I listen to music. If that’s you, the Galaxy Buds Plus might be a better choice since they seal in your ears and create more isolation, even without any active noise cancellation. If you buy the Galaxy Buds Live because of the advertised noise canceling, you’ll be sorely underwhelmed and disappointed.
  Bluetooth stability on the Buds Live doesn’t seem quite as robust as with the Galaxy Buds Plus. I’ve had the occasional bout of split-second cutouts when walking down the street. It’s not a constant source of frustration, but it happens with more frequency than other earbuds I’ve reviewed lately. I’ve had zero signal problems when relaxing and listening at my apartment, though. Nor do I have any gripes with battery life; Samsung says the Buds Live can last for up to six hours of continuous listening with ANC enabled (21 hours total with case recharges) or eight with it off (29 hours with the case). Those numbers are quite good and beat out the AirPods Pro, but they fall short of the staggering 11 straight hours the Galaxy Buds Plus can last on a charge.
  With three total beamforming mics — two on the exterior and one inside — the Galaxy Buds Live have performed just fine during my Zoom meetings and voice calls. Samsung says they also have a “voice pickup unit” in the hardware for improved speech detection. You can use either bud independently while the other is charging in the case.
  So there’s a lot of good, but the Galaxy Buds Live aren’t without faults. For one, they’re only rated IPX2 for water and sweat resistance, which is less durable than wireless earbuds from other manufacturers in 2020. I’d be a little worried about them in the rain or if you sweat a lot at the gym. These aren’t destined to be fitness buds, which is disappointing since they’ve got a design that helps you stay aware when out running. And like most other true wireless earbuds besides those from Jabra, the Buds Live can only connect to a single device at a time.
  Taken as a whole package, the Galaxy Buds Live exceeded what I came in expecting based on the leaks and the uncertainty I had about what the bean shape would mean for comfort and sound quality. Neither wound up being a concern after actually using them. If you’re okay living with a healthy dose of outside sound when listening to the Galaxy Buds Live, there’s a lot to like.
title: 三星Galaxy Buds直播点评：平衡音效大胆、非常规设计但消噪效果不佳偶有信号干扰
title_en: Samsung Galaxy Buds Live review: balanced sound with a bold, unconventional design but noise cancellation isn't good and there is occasional signal interference

note:
Android手机上的臃肿软件已经存在了很长时间，特别是在三星和华为手机上，以至于这已经不再是什么新闻了。尽管如此，当我们发现OnePlus参与了这种可疑的做法时，我们还是感到非常惊讶。被质疑的OxygenOS膨胀软件可能来自其中最可疑的公司-Facebook。从OnePlus 8系列开始，再到Nord，用户不得不在他们的全新手机上忍受一堆Facebook拥有的应用程序和一些后台服务，其中一些无法卸载。
这引起了我们的注意，因为XDA的马克斯·温巴赫(Max Weinbach)在推特上发布了几张Instagram应用程序更新的截图，这些截图是通过Facebook应用程序管理器服务进行更新的，而不是像你想象的那样通过Play Store进行更新。Reddit上的多位人士指出，这种情况已经有一段时间了。我们检查了几款OnePlus手机，果不其然，该公司最新的三款手机都预装了Facebook的系统应用程序和服务，作为操作系统的一部分。似乎你会在所有安装OxygenOS的OnePlus手机发货的地区都能找到它们-所以，几乎所有地方都有，除了中国。
我们联系了OnePlus寻求评论，他们向我们证实，OnePlus8、8Pro和Nord都包括Facebook App Installer、Facebook App Manager和Facebook Services作为系统应用。主要的Facebook应用程序Messenger和Instagram以及Netflix也在场。在回应Ideas活动中的一项功能建议时，OnePlus的理由是，预装这些应用程序可以确保Facebook上更高的电池效率，并增强Netflix上的HDR播放-你会怎么做就怎么做吧。
正如Max指出的那样，如果你的手机上安装了这些服务，Facebook旗下的应用程序可能会通过这些服务而不是Play Store来获取更新。这些系统服务也是使用后台数据发现的，尽管数量很少。考虑到与Facebook共享的信息类型完全不清楚，这可能令人担忧。
您可以卸载面向用户的Facebook、Messenger和Instagram应用程序，但不能卸载后台服务。然而，禁用它们是可能的，这样做不会损坏您手机上的任何东西。OnePlus表示，它将继续在欧洲、印度和北美销售的手机上安装这些Facebook应用程序和服务。值得庆幸的是，旧手机(OnePlus 7T及更早版本)不会将这些应用程序强制作为操作系统更新的一部分。虽然不像三星在一个用户界面上投放广告那样明目张胆，但OnePlus与Facebook这样的公司的合作确实让很多人感到惊讶，OnePlus手机的用户对此感到不安是可以理解的。
note_en:
Bloatware on Android phones has been around for so long — especially on  Samsung and Huawei phones — that it's hardly even news anymore. Even so, it was a huge surprise when we found out that OnePlus has involved itself in this dubious practice. The OxygenOS bloatware in question comes from probably the shadiest company of them all — Facebook. Starting with the OnePlus 8 series and continuing with the Nord, users have had to put up with a bunch of Facebook-owned apps and a few background services on their brand-new handsets, some of which cannot be uninstalled.
  This came to our attention when Max Weinbach of XDA  tweeted a couple of screenshots of the Instagram app updating via the Facebook App Manager service instead of through Play Store as you'd expect. A number of people on  Reddit pointed out that this has been the case for some time. We checked several OnePlus phones, and sure enough, the company's three most recent models come with system apps and services from Facebook preinstalled as part of the OS. It seems you'll find them in all regions where OnePlus phones ship with OxygenOS — so, pretty much everywhere but China.
   We reached out to OnePlus for comment and they confirmed to us that the OnePlus 8, 8 Pro, and Nord all include the Facebook App Installer, Facebook App Manager, and Facebook Services as system apps. The main Facebook app, Messenger, and Instagram are also present, as well as Netflix. In  response to a feature suggestion under its  Ideas campaign, OnePlus reasoned that the pre-installation of these apps ensures better battery efficiency on Facebook and enhances HDR playback on Netflix — make of that what you will.
   As  Max points out, if you’ve got these services on your phone, Facebook-owned apps may pull updates via these services instead of the Play Store. These system services have been spotted using data in the background, too, albeit in small quantities. That's potentially alarming considering there’s absolutely no clarity on the kind of information being shared with Facebook.
    You can uninstall the user-facing Facebook, Messenger, and Instagram apps, but not the background services. It is possible to disable them, however, and doing so won't break anything on your phone. OnePlus says it will continue to install these Facebook apps and services on phones sold in Europe, India, and North America. Thankfully, older phones (OnePlus 7T and before) won't have these apps forced on them as part of an OS update. Though not as blatant as Samsung  placing ads all over One UI, OnePlus’s partnership with a company like Facebook does raise more than a few eyebrows, and owners of OnePlus phones are  understandably upset by this.
title: OnePlus在OnePlus 8和Nord系列手机上预装了Facebook膨胀软件，这些软件无法作为操作系统的一部分卸载
title_en: OnePlus is preloading OnePlus 8 and Nord series phones with Facebook bloatware that can't be uninstalled as part of the OS

note:
谷歌已经停止生产去年10月发布的旗舰手机Pixel4和Pixel4XL。这两款设备在美国的谷歌商店都脱销了，尽管一些变种目前在其他地区仍然可以买到。
一位谷歌发言人向The Verge证实：“谷歌商店已经售出了Pixel4[和]4XL的库存，并完成了销售。”“对于那些仍然有兴趣购买Pixel4[和]4XL的人来说，有些合作伙伴可以提供该产品，而供货可以持续。就像所有Pixel设备一样，Pixel 4将在至少三年内继续获得软件和安全更新，从该设备首次在美国的谷歌商店上市之日起算。“。
谷歌这么快就停产Pixel手机是不寻常的。Pixels 2和Pixels 3各自的销售时间都在18个月左右，谷歌在推出它们的继任者大约6个月后停止了销售。此举意味着，从技术上讲，谷歌不再有旗舰手机可供销售。
不过，这只是个技术细节-谷歌本周早些时候推出了新的Pixel4A，将于8月20日发布，并证实Pixel5和配备5G的4A将于今年秋天晚些时候发布。虽然4A是一款经济型手机，但在电池续航时间更长，边框设计更少，主摄像头同样好的情况下，你可能会更喜欢它，而不是普通的Pixel 4。这是迪特尔在我们的评论中得出的结论，他说：“Pixel 4A比其他Pixel手机更有说服力。”
note_en:
Google has discontinued  the Pixel 4 and Pixel 4 XL, its flagship phones that were released in October of last year. Both devices are out of stock in Google’s store in the US, though some variants are still available in other regions for the time being.
 “Google Store has sold through its inventory and completed sales of Pixel 4 [and] 4 XL,” a Google spokesperson confirms to  The Verge. “For people who are still interested in buying Pixel 4 [and] 4 XL, the product is available from some partners while supplies last. Just like all Pixel devices, Pixel 4 will continue to get software and security updates for at least three years from when the device first became available on the Google Store in the US.”
 It’s unusual for Google to discontinue a Pixel phone so quickly. The Pixels  2 and  3 were on sale for around 18 months each, with Google stopping sales roughly six months after the introduction of their successors. The move means Google technically no longer has a flagship phone for sale.
 That is a technicality, though — Google introduced the new Pixel 4A earlier this week for an August 20th release, and also confirmed that  the Pixel 5 and a 5G-equipped 4A would be coming later this fall. And while the 4A is a budget device, between its better battery life, less bezel-y design, and equally good main camera you might actually prefer it to the regular Pixel 4. That’s the conclusion Dieter came to in  our review, saying “the Pixel 4A makes a much stronger case for itself than other Pixel phones.”
title: 谷歌已停产去年10月发布的旗舰手机Pixel4和Pixel4XL
title_en: Google has discontinued the Pixel 4 and Pixel 4 XL, its flagship phones that were released in October of last year

note:
在宣布Pixel4a的几天后，谷歌悄悄地停止了Pixel4和Pixel4XL的销售。Verge早些时候就注意到了这一举动，这代表着谷歌旗舰产品的生命周期极其缩短-在该公司继续销售两款前身的18个月中，大约有一半的时间是这样的。
谷歌已经宣布Pixel5即将上市，当时它指出，即将推出的这款手机将是Pixel5G的两款Pixels设备之一，另外还有Pixel4a 5G。
该公司告诉TechCrunch：“谷歌商店已经售出了其库存，并完成了Pixel4|4XL的销售。”“对于那些仍有兴趣购买Pixel 4|4XL的人来说，有些合作伙伴会提供该产品，而供应期则会持续。就像所有的Pixel设备一样，Pixel 4将在至少3年内继续获得软件和安全更新，从该设备首次在美国的谷歌商店上市之日起算。“。
Pixel4在很大程度上是一款广受欢迎的设备，这主要归功于令人印象深刻的摄像工作。但这款手机受到电池续航时间不佳的阻碍-谷歌后来在4a中解决了这一问题。这款新的经济型手机在价位上也配备了出色的摄像头，这使得Pixel4的存在有些多余。虽然Pixel4XL的终结确实给谷歌留下了更大的选择。
该公司显然一直在用其智能手机应对一种身份危机。最近的管理层改组似乎表明，该公司希望为销售不均衡的产品线找到新的方向。在其他方面，谷歌进入了一个已经饱和的市场，在将其产品与其他Android手机区分开来方面遇到了一些困难。
Pixel 5是否会成为第一款受益于该部门新方向的设备，还有待观察。
note_en:
Days after  announcing the Pixel 4a, Google has quietly discontinued sales of the  Pixel 4 and Pixel 4 XL. The move, noted early by  the Verge, represents an extremely truncated lifecycle for a Google flagship — around half of the 18 months the company continued to sell its two predecessors.
 Google already announced the imminent arrival of the Pixel 5, when it noted the forthcoming handset would be one of two Pixels devices  to sport 5G, along with the Pixel 4a 5G.
 “Google Store has sold through its inventory and completed sales of Pixel 4|4XL,” the company tells TechCrunch. “For people who are still interested in buying Pixel 4|4XL, the product is available from some partners while supplies last. Just like all Pixel devices, Pixel 4 will continue to get software and security updates for at least 3 years from when the device first became available on the Google Store in the U.S.”
  The Pixel 4 was a largely well-received device, owing mostly to impressive camera work. But the handset was hampered by bad battery life — something Google has since addressed in the 4a. The new budget handset also sports an excellent camera for its price point, making the Pixel 4’s   existence somewhat redundant. Though the end of the Pixel 4 XL does leave Google with a larger option.
 The company has clearly been dealing with a kind of identity crisis with its smartphones. A recent management shakeup appears to point to a desire for a new direction for the line, which has long suffered from uneven sales. Among other things, Google entered an already saturated market and has had some trouble distinguishing its offerings from other Android handsets.
 It remains to be seen whether the Pixel 5 will be the first device to benefit from the division’s new direction.
title: 谷歌在Pixel4发布9个月后停止销售
title_en: Google discontinues the Pixel 4, nine months after release

note:
大量病毒式的Google Sheet和Docs打破了对可扩展协作工具的需求。
在短暂的一段时间里，全国各地惊慌失措的国际学生在谷歌表格中找到了希望。
当美国移民和海关执法局(ICE)在7月6日宣布，没有注册亲自参加课程的国际学生可能会在秋季面临驱逐出境时，Facebook上支持不同移民背景学生的H-4 Hope群组的创始人苏马纳·卡卢瓦伊(Sumana Kaluvai)建立了一个系统，将国际学生与愿意放弃课程席位的同龄人联系起来，这些课程可能会赋予他们的同学留在美国的权利。她使用了她触手可及的最近工具Google Sheets来促进这些课程交流，并开始在社交媒体上传播这些资源。
她的电子表格很快就像病毒一样传播开来，吸引了大量的流量，使其反应迟钝。加州大学伯克利分校(University of California，Berkeley)数据科学专业的学生麦克莱恩·蒂尔(McClain Thiel)最终伸出援手，提出建立一个网站来取代谷歌的工作表，并在7月9日推出了Support Our International Students。虽然ICE会在几天后撤销这项政策，但他们的新网站设法缓解了最初谷歌表格遇到的问题。
Kaluvai在使用Google Sheet进行组织方面的经验和挑战并不是独一无二的。在过去的十年里，谷歌的协作工具套件在社会运动和其他形式的广泛协作中稳步获得了突出的地位。它被用来在2011年组织占领华尔街运动，为2016年美国大选后的抗议传播资源，并收集对2017年加州野火的回应。在2020年期间，这些工具赢得了“抵抗运动的社交媒体”的美誉；它们在大流行互助组织的形成、“黑人生命也是命”(Black Lives Matter)运动的组织以及博彩业#MeToo清算中的指控汇总方面发挥了关键作用。
但当这些资源像病毒一样传播时，他们往往会遇到G Suite的限制。爱德华·萨皮里亚说：“每当你加载页面时，一半的时间都会失败。”他最初使用谷歌文档创建了冠状病毒技术手册，这是一个众包目录，里面有新冠肺炎响应的工具、服务和资源。
病毒式的Google Sheet和Google Docs That Break的激增是一个迹象，表明协作已经超出了我们可以立即使用的协作工具的范围。随着组织者和贡献者的人数不断扩大，这些项目的规模也呈爆炸式增长，公民日常可以用来带头开展这些努力的工具尚未迎头赶上。
谷歌文档和谷歌工作表最早是在十多年前建立的，允许个人“从其他人那里获得反馈和贡献[…]。而不必通过电子邮件发送文件副本。“。它们旨在促进我们可能通过电子邮件合理尝试的那种协作，而不是广泛的资源和运动。一个Google支持页面声明“最多100个有查看、编辑或评论权限的人可以同时处理Google Docs、Sheet或幻灯片文件”，并有一个部分专门对与许多人共享后变得反应迟钝的文件进行故障排除，并认识到常见的陷阱。
谷歌在这方面并不孤单；与之竞争的协作编辑工具Dropbox Paper也对并发合作者的数量施加了限制。
乔纳森·罗谢尔(Jonathan Rochelle)在给OneZero的一封电子邮件中表示，他和他的团队发现，一个文档有超过25到50名编辑，人们很难进行有效的协作。乔纳森·罗谢尔曾在2005年至2013年担任谷歌协作工具套件的首席产品经理。在加入谷歌之前，罗谢尔创立了2Web Technologies，这是一家谷歌收购的公司，其面向大型企业的基于Web的电子表格产品后来发展成为Google Sheets。罗谢尔花了数年时间构建协作工具，他说扩展这些工具的问题不一定是技术本身。当大型群体试图围绕现实生活中的共同目标进行动员时，结构和组织是必要的，即使互动是在网上进行的，这一点也是正确的。可以容纳大量贡献者的协作工具的设计方式需要提供足够的指导，以便人们可以轻松地理解如何贡献，但又不能太多，以至于他们感觉受到了限制。根据罗谢尔的经验，当许多人试图编辑同一份文档时，“社会规范占据了主导地位”。“人们尽量避开彼此--就像人们避免在会议上互相议论一样。”同时，当几十个人试图编辑同一文档时，他们也可能同时尝试编辑相同的单元格或文本行。谷歌对并发贡献者数量的限制可以帮助降低用户遇到这类可用性挑战的可能性。
考虑到当今最广为人知的协作工具的局限性，在未来，Kaluvai能够快速轻松地创建她的班级交换系统，而Saperia他的众包手册将需要更复杂的工具。
通向这一未来有两条不同的道路：一条专注于完善协作工具本身，而另一条则努力赋予个人为其事业构建定制解决方案的自主权。
这些工具可能发展的第一种方式是提供专门为促进大规模协作和信息收集而设计的功能。在建立了冠状病毒技术手册之后，伦敦民用技术中心院长萨皮里亚开始与他的同事们合作开发一种为大规模合作而设计的工具。他们创建了Docs Plus，这是一个由Nesta和Grant for the Web资助的项目，该项目利用支持更好的组织、社区建设等功能来增强文档格式，并从其他生产力工具(如Zoom、Slake和MediaWiki)中汲取灵感。Saperia在描述Google Docs的优势时说：“Google Docs向我们展示了看起来像文档的东西是令人信服的。”“人们能理解它们，编辑它们不会有太多问题。”
另一种克服Google Sheet局限性的方法不是建立一个更好的平台，而是想象一个拥有更好地支持公民将他们的想法变成现实的技术的未来，在这种情况下，Kaluvai自己能够构建一个定制的应用程序，像她创建Google Sheet一样容易地促进班级交换。这一现实--一个人不需要特殊技能就能构建软件的现实--正是无代码运动所追求的。
在过去的十年里，Webflow和Bubble等公司已经形成了创造可视化工具的雄心，任何人-包括特别是那些没有编程背景的人-都可以创建强大的网站和应用程序。Webflow的联合创始人弗拉德·马格达林(Vlad Magdalin)说，今天构建软件是“一种不必要的复杂和不必要的排他性的专业手艺和贸易”。无代码工具允许人们使用可视化的拖放界面而不是代码来构建网站和应用程序。就像Squapace和Wix这样的平台可以让人们在不了解HTML和CSS的情况下启动网站一样，Webflow和Bubble这样的平台可以让人们创建交互式和动态的网站，而不必了解发出网络请求或与数据库交互的机制。
G Suite稳步增加了一些功能，这在某种程度上是按照无代码精神设计的。例如，通过将Google表单与包含表单响应的可公开查看的Google表单配对，您已经拼凑出一个基本的网站，它允许您在不编写一行代码的情况下收集和显示信息。事实上，如果Kaluvai从一开始就使用Google表单和仅供查看的Google表单实现班级交换，那么她可能已经避免了面临的一些困难。然而，为了完全接受无代码的精神，Google的工具必须经过设计、信任和用户理解，以便为他们关心的事业创建和部署强大的自定义解决方案。在他们目前的状态下
如果任何使用网络的人也能为其创造，我们可能会开始侵蚀帮助延续存在的经济差距的排他性力量，部分原因是有限的一部分人口知道如何使用软件构建解决方案和创造价值。
当来自越南的加州大学洛杉矶分校(UCLA)学生斯特拉·阮(Stella Nguyen)看到卡鲁瓦伊的电子表格时，她“发现许多学生--无论是国际学生还是非国际学生--都在一起，这让她感到欣慰。”Google Docs帮助我们走到了这个时代，在这个时代，任何能创建和编辑文档的人都能感觉到自己有能力帮助他人，培养希望和联系。现在，我们只需要像我们一样雄心勃勃的工具。
note_en:
A sea of viral Google Sheets and Docs that break attests to the need for scalable collaboration tools
   For a brief period, panicking international students across the nation found hope in a Google Sheet.
 When the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agency announced on July 6 that  international students who weren’t enrolled in courses meeting in-person could face deportation in the fall, Sumana Kaluvai — the creator of H-4 Hope, a Facebook group that supports students of varying immigration backgrounds — built a system for connecting international students with peers who were willing to surrender their seats in courses that could grant their classmates the right to stay in the country. She used the closest tool in her reach, Google Sheets, to facilitate these class exchanges and began circulating the resource on social media.
 Her spreadsheet quickly went viral, attracting levels of traffic that rendered it unresponsive. McClain Thiel, a data science student at the University of California, Berkeley, eventually reached out and offered to build a website to replace the Google Sheet, and on July 9, they launched  Support Our International Students. Though ICE would rescind the policy days later, their new website managed to mitigate the problems the original Google Sheet encountered.
 Kaluvai’s experiences and challenges with using Google Sheets to organize are not unique. In the past decade, Google’s suite of collaborative tools has steadily gained prominence in social movements and other forms of widespread collaboration. It was used to  organize Occupy Wall Street movements in 2011, disseminate resources for  protesting after the U.S. election in 2016, and assemble  response to the California wildfires in 2017. During 2020, these tools have earned a reputation as “ the social media of the resistance;” they have played a key role in the  formation of pandemic mutual aid groups, the  organization of the Black Lives Matter movement, and the aggregation of allegations in the  gaming industry’s #MeToo reckoning.
 But when these resources go viral, they often encounter limitations of G Suite. “Whenever you loaded the page, it would just fail half the time,” says Edward Saperia, who initially used Google Docs to build  Coronavirus Tech Handbook, a crowdsourced directory of tools, services, and resources for Covid-19 response.
 The proliferation of viral Google Sheets and Google Docs that break is a sign that collaboration has outgrown the collaboration tools at our immediate disposal. As the demographic of organizers and contributors has broadened and the scale of these projects has exploded, tools everyday citizens can use to spearhead these efforts have yet to catch up.
 Google Docs and Google Sheets were first built  more than a decade ago to allow individuals to “get feedback and contributions from others […] without having to email around copies of files.” They were designed to facilitate the kind of collaboration we might reasonably attempt via email — not widespread resources and movements. A Google  support page states that “up to 100 people with view, edit, or comment permissions can work on a Google Docs, Sheets, or Slides file at the same time” and has a section devoted to troubleshooting files that become unresponsive after being shared with many people, recognizing the common pitfall.
 Google is not alone in this regard; Dropbox Paper, a competing collaborative editing tool,  also imposes a limit on the number of concurrent collaborators.
 In an email to  OneZero, Jonathan Rochelle, the lead product manager for Google’s suite of collaborative tools from 2005 to 2013, said that he and his team found that having more than 25 to 50 editors on a single document made it hard for people to collaborate effectively. Before joining Google, Rochelle founded 2Web Technologies, a company Google acquired whose web-based spreadsheet products for large enterprises later grew into Google Sheets. Having spent years building collaborative tools, Rochelle says the problem with scaling them isn’t necessarily about the technology itself. When large groups attempt to mobilize around common goals in real life, structure and organization are necessary, and this holds true even when the interactions are conducted online. Collaborative tools that can accommodate large numbers of contributors will need to be designed in a way that offers enough guidance that people can easily understand how to contribute, but not too much that they feel restricted. In Rochelle’s experience, “social norms take over” when many people attempt to edit the same document. “People try to get out of each other’s way — the same way people avoid talking over each other in meetings.” At the same time, when dozens of people try to edit the same document, they may also simultaneously try to edit the same cell or line of text. Google’s limits on the number of concurrent contributors can help reduce the likelihood of users encountering these sorts of usability challenges.
  Considering the limitations of today’s most widely known tools for collaboration, a future where Kaluvai is able to quickly and easily create her class exchange system and Saperia his crowdsourced handbook will require more sophisticated tools.
 There are two different paths to this future: One focuses on perfecting tooling for collaboration itself while the other strives to grant individuals the autonomy to build custom solutions for their causes.
 The first way these tools could evolve is by offering features designed specifically to foster mass collaboration and information gathering. After building the Coronavirus Tech Handbook, Saperia, who is dean of the London hub for civic technology  Newspeak House, began working with his colleagues on a tool designed for collaboration at scale. They created  Docs Plus, a project funded by  Nesta and  Grant for the Web that augments the document format with features that support better organization, community building, and more, drawing inspiration from other productivity tools, such as Zoom, Slack, and MediaWiki. “Google Docs has shown us that things that look like documents are compelling,” Saperia said when describing the advantages of Google Docs. “People understand them and can edit them without too many problems.”
 Another approach to overcoming the limits of Google Sheets is not to build a better platform, but to imagine a future with technology that better supports citizens in bringing their ideas to life, one in which Kaluvai herself is able to build a custom application to facilitate class swapping as easily as she can create a Google Sheet. This reality — one where someone can build software without special skills — is what the no-code movement is chasing.
  Over the last decade, companies such as Webflow and Bubble have formed with aspirations to create visual tools that allow anyone — including and especially those without programming backgrounds — to create powerful websites and applications. Building software today, Webflow’s co-founder Vlad Magdalin says, is “a specialized craft and trade that is unnecessarily complex and unnecessarily exclusionary.” No-code tools allow people to build websites and applications using visual drag-and-drop interfaces instead of code. In the same way platforms such as Squarespace and Wix enable people to launch websites without understanding HTML and CSS, platforms such as Webflow and Bubble allow people to create interactive and dynamic websites without having to understand the mechanics of making network requests or interacting with databases.
 G Suite has steadily added features somewhat designed in the spirit of no-code. By pairing a Google Form with a publicly viewable Google Sheet containing the form’s responses, for instance, you’ve tossed together a basic website that allows you to collect and display information without writing a single line of code. In fact, Kaluvai may have been able to avoid some of the difficulties she faced had she implemented the class exchange using a Google Form and view-only Google Sheet in the first place. However, to fully embrace the spirit of no-code, Google’s tools would have to be designed, trusted, and understood by their users to be for the creation and deployment of powerful custom solutions to the causes they care about. In their current state, they are not; documents become unresponsive with high levels of traffic, privacy concerns with Google and its products  continue to grow, and the Google Form plus Google Sheet solution is not obvious, even for someone who is comfortable using technology.
 If anyone who uses the web could also create for it, we might begin eroding the exclusionary forces that help perpetuate the economic disparity that exists in part because a limited segment of the population understands how to build solutions and create value using software.
 When Stella Nguyen, a UCLA student from Vietnam, came across Kaluvai’s spreadsheet, she “found it comforting that many students — international or not — were coming together.” Google Docs has helped get us here, to an era where anyone who can create and edit a document can feel empowered to help others and foster hope and connection. Now, we just need tools that are as ambitious as we are.

title_en: Social Movements Are Pushing Google Sheets to the Breaking Point
